serviceName: Core Banking Resilience – Disaster Recovery Capability
purpose:
Provides a defined, tested disaster recovery capability for core banking platforms to ensure service continuity during major outages or site failures.
Establishes clear RTO/RPO targets, recovery patterns, and governance aligned to regulatory and customer resilience expectations.
consumers: All core banking customers hosted by Experteq
currentStack:
Platform-dependent DR approaches
Mixed active/passive and restore-based recovery patterns
Limited standardisation and inconsistent testing cadence
constraints:
DR designs vary by customer and platform
Recovery objectives not consistently defined or tested
High cost and complexity without a standard reference architecture
targetState:
A standardised set of DR reference architectures for core banking platforms.
Defines clear recovery tiers (e.g. restore-based, warm standby, active/active) with aligned RTO/RPO targets.
Includes documented runbooks, testing schedules, and governance oversight.
targetDrivers:
APRA CPS 230 business continuity and service resilience
Customer expectations for banking service availability
Operational risk reduction and predictable recovery outcomes

risks.description: Extended outage of core banking services during a major incident
Inability to demonstrate DR readiness to regulators or customers

actions.component: Define DR Recovery Tiers and Objectives
actions.notes: Define standard DR tiers with target RTO/RPO aligned to platform criticality
Design repeatable DR architecture patterns for core banking platforms
Implement and validate DR capability for a selected platform
Define testing cadence, evidence capture, and governance reporting
Progressive adoption of standard DR capability across core banking customers
